title: Graceful Boundaries non-success response
description: Core structured refusal shape for all non-success HTTP responses (400, 401, 403, 404, 405, 410, 422, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504) per Graceful Boundaries. Spec: https://gracefulboundaries.dev/spec

error.description: Stable machine-parseable error category in snake_case, e.g. rate_limit_exceeded, invalid_input, not_found.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation of what happened and how to proceed.

why.description: The security, policy, or operational reason this response exists. Must explain the purpose, not restate the error.

limit.description: The limit that was exceeded, in human-readable form. Required for 429 responses (see refusal-429.schema.json).

retryAfterSeconds.description: Seconds until the caller can retry. Required for 429 responses; optional for availability errors.

limitId.description: Stable identifier for the specific limit exceeded.

limitType.description: Type of the limit exceeded, such as ip-rate or cost-limit.

scope.description: Subject of the exceeded limit, such as ip, key, user, resource, or global.

windowResetAt.description: ISO timestamp or Unix timestamp for the window reset.

cached.description: Whether a cached result is available.

cachedResultUrl.description: URL to retrieve the cached result. Must be a relative path or same-origin absolute URL (SC-6).

alternativeEndpoint.description: A different endpoint that may serve the need. Must be a relative path or same-origin absolute URL (SC-6).

field.description: Which input field failed validation (input class).

expected.description: What valid input looks like, stated positively (SC-3).

allowedMethods.description: Which HTTP methods are accepted (405 responses).

scanAvailable.description: Whether the caller can create the missing resource (not-found class).

scanUrl.description: Endpoint to create the missing resource. Not a trust bypass (SC-8).

statusUrl.description: Status page or health check endpoint (availability class).
